tool.name: inforesidue
tool.summary: Report deterministic metadata for one canonical amino-acid residue

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `inforesidue` reports one deterministic metadata row for a single canonical amino-acid residue. The EMBOSS-RS v1 implementation focuses on stable naming, mass, hydropathy, and coarse biochemical classes rather than reproducing multiple historical report styles.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts exactly one canonical amino-acid residue symbol. The v1 surface supports the standard twenty residues and rejects ambiguous symbols such as `X`.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits a stable one-row table with columns `residue`, `three_letter`, `name`, `charge_class`, `polarity_class`, `average_mass`, and `hydropathy`.

sections.title: Property Model
sections.content: Average mass uses the same residue-mass table used by `pepstats`. Hydropathy uses the same Kyte-Doolittle scale used by `pepwindow`. `charge_class` and `polarity_class` are governed coarse classes rather than historical AAINDEX identifiers.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs inforesidue`. Validation currently covers canonical-residue lookup, stable one-row table emission, and rejection of unsupported residue symbols.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not expose the broader historical biochemical or structural annotation surface. It is a stable lookup over one governed residue-property table.
